cold_open.hook: Parker, the fintech startup that was supposed to kill traditional banking, just filed for bankruptcy—proving disruption is a lot harder when you actually have to make money.

markets.macro_note: Chip stocks ripping, consumer discretionary stumbling—the market's choosing violence on burger margins today.

big_story.story_title: Paul Tudor Jones Warns Trump-Era Market Boom Could End in a 35% Crash. Here's Why He's Still Buying
big_story.angle: A legendary macro trader is simultaneously predicting a third of the market's value vanishes AND buying—the cognitive dissonance is the story, not the crash prediction itself.

quick_hits.angle: NRG Energy missed earnings but reaffirmed guidance anyway—this is the corporate equivalent of saying 'I'm fine' while bleeding.
Cathie Wood's still hopeful on Tesla while the stock's up 4%—her conviction is either unshakeable or she's contractually obligated to say this.
Rubio and Witkoff are in Miami meeting a Qatari mediator on Iran—this is either diplomacy or the world's most expensive real estate transaction.
A Labour MP just gave Starmer's cabinet a Monday ultimatum—British politics is now operating on Slack deadlines.

odd_thing.joke_angle: Iran demanding guarantees for World Cup participation is the most Iran thing ever—'we'll play if you promise not to sanction us mid-tournament.'

yesterday_callback.topic: Parker, the fintech startup that was supposed to disrupt banking, just filed for bankruptcy
yesterday_callback.fresh_take: Yesterday we said Parker proved disruption requires actual revenue—today we're watching the entire fintech thesis get stress-tested as rates stay elevated and venture capital remembers what profitability means.
